title: Replicate: GenAI Inference API
description: Access 1000+ open-source AI models via unified API; pay-per-second pricing; strong for prototyping and diverse model selection.

cost.costNotes: Cost-effective for open-source models. Cold starts add latency but not cost.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Pay-per-second pricing ($0.00002-$0.01/sec) creates highly variable costs. Min ($5): Prototyping/testing with ~250-500 API calls using lightweight models like Whisper or small LLMs (5-10 seconds each). Max ($2000): Production use with 20,000-50,000 generations monthly using mid-range models (Stable Diffusion, SDXL, Llama 70B) averaging 10-20 seconds per request. Heavy enterprise usage with SOTA models could exceed $5000/mo. No cold start costs, only generation time charged.
provenance.adoptionCost: Medium-low complexity. Simple REST API with Python/Node.js SDKs reduces integration effort (2-4 developer days). Key costs: (1) API integration and error handling: 1-2 days, (2) Model selection and testing across 1000+ options: 1-2 days, (3) Prompt engineering and output parsing for chosen models: 1 day. Straightforward documentation and standard HTTP patterns lower barrier. Complexity comes from choosing appropriate models and handling model-specific input/output formats rather than API mechanics.
provenance.licenseCost: Pure pay-per-use consumption model with no upfront licensing, subscription fees, or minimum commitments. New users receive free credits ($10-50) for initial testing. No enterprise license required - costs scale linearly with usage. Open-source models accessible through platform avoid individual model licensing concerns. Only ongoing runtime costs apply, making this $0 for license/subscription category.

provides.reasoning: Core capability - this pattern provides access to AI model inference through Replicate's API across multiple model types.
Replicate supports image generation models like Stable Diffusion and DALL-E variants for creating images from text prompts.
Replicate provides access to text and LLM models for generation tasks including chat, completion, and text transformation.
Replicate provides access to audio generation models for tasks like speech synthesis and music generation.
Replicate supports video generation models for creating and manipulating video content through AI.

requires.reasoning: Replicate provides a unified REST API that requires HTTP client functionality to make requests to their endpoints for model inference.
API key/token storage required to authenticate with Replicate's API securely.
Replicate uses asynchronous processing for model inference - jobs are queued and polled for completion, requiring async workflow management.
Replicate supports webhook notifications for job completion as an alternative to polling, enabling event-driven workflows.
Replicate has usage limits and per-second billing that requires applications to implement rate limiting and quota management logic.

supports_nfr.reason: Replicate's shared multi-tenant SaaS infrastructure provides maximum availability of 99% (two nines).
GenAI inference involves complex model computation with minimum p95 latency of 500ms due to processing overhead.
P99 latencies for GenAI inference are typically 1000ms or higher due to cold starts and model loading.
Replicate's managed service with automatic failover and health monitoring supports RTO of 5 minutes or greater.
Pattern maximum throughput is 36M jobs/hour (10K requests/second) based on enterprise tier rate limits.
Replicate does not offer a HIPAA BAA; models run on shared GPU infrastructure not suitable for PHI.
Replicate lacks SOX-compliant audit and data residency controls.

supports_constraints.reason: Replicate is a cloud-based SaaS service and cannot be deployed on-premises.
Replicate provides REST API accessible from any language, with official SDKs for Python, TypeScript, and JavaScript.
Replicate API can be called from any platform type that supports HTTP REST clients including web, mobile, and backend services.
Pattern is specifically for Replicate service; only applicable when Replicate is listed in acceptable SaaS providers.
Pattern is specifically designed for AI/ML inference workloads using Replicate's model serving platform.

provider.description: GenAI inference provider - Replicate offers 1000+ open-source models with pay-per-second pricing

pricing_model.description: Pricing approach: pay-per-second for sporadic usage and prototyping, or dedicated deployments for consistent production workloads with reserved capacity

model_category.description: Primary model category to use - determines which open-source models are available and pricing structure

timeout_seconds.description: Maximum time to wait for model inference completion before timing out (some models like video generation take longer)

webhook_enabled.description: Enable webhooks for async predictions - recommended for long-running models to avoid blocking HTTP connections

service_auth_method.description: Service credential method used to authenticate to the LLM provider API. Always api_key — stored in environment variable, never in code.

warn_nfr.reason: Replicate's pay-per-second SaaS pricing and API overhead make it suboptimal for very low-volume inference (<10 jobs/hour).
warn_nfr.message: genai-inference--replicate: peak throughput is {actual} jobs/hour (<10). Replicate's SaaS overhead and per-second billing may be inefficient for very low-volume workloads; consider local model hosting or batch processing.

warn_nfr.reason: Replicate processes data on external servers. Sending PII to third-party APIs may violate compliance requirements (GDPR).
warn_nfr.message: genai-inference--replicate: spec indicates PII handling, but Replicate API processes data externally which may violate data residency or compliance requirements.
